title: The Laud Troy Book
summary: The pinned repository XML represents U-M CME item APE9595.0001.001, an OCR-derived encoding of J. Ernst Wülfing's 1902-1903 EETS edition of the complete 18,664-line Laud Troy Book. Wülfing transcribed the unique witness, Bodleian MS. Laud Misc. 595. The abstract Middle English work directly adapts Guido delle Colonne's Historia destructionis Troiae; possible direct use of Benoît's Roman de Troie remains tentative. The graph distinguishes repository artifact, raw package, digital encoding, edition facsimiles and carriers, scholarly edition, abstract works, manuscript witness, catalog records, and modern reprint descriptions. Fifty-two fixity-verified source-cache captures are linked to their exact access routes; six derived audits remain evidence-only.

entities.label: General Corpus pinned APE9595 artifact
entities.description: The checked-in CME-derived XML repository representation at pinned submodule commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.

entities.label: Internet Archive copy of the complete U-M APE9595 raw package
entities.description: A 585-member ZIP container holding 580 TIFFs plus XML, TXT, header, load log, and page-view data; not itself the encoding or facsimile.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME encoding APE9595.0001.001
entities.description: Uncorrected OCR-derived TEI-Libraries-Level-1 XML created in 2006 with linked page images.

entities.label: University of Michigan 580-TIFF facsimile of the bound Wülfing edition carrier
entities.description: Complete 600-dpi monochrome page-image set of the U-M print carrier, including both edition parts and one embedded printed phototype; not a manuscript facsimile.
entities.reproduction_format: 580 1-bit CCITT Group 4 TIFFs, 3296 x 5088 pixels, 600 dpi

entities.label: University of Michigan bound carrier of EETS OS 121-122
entities.description: The physical print carrier captured in the 580 U-M TIFFs; it contains Wülfing's two publication parts in one bound object.

entities.label: J. Ernst Wülfing, The Laud Troy Book, EETS OS 121-122 (1902-1903)
entities.description: The two-part first edition of all 18,664 numbered lines; the advertised introduction, notes, and glossary were never published as a third part.

entities.label: The Laud Troy Book (abstract medieval work)
entities.description: The anonymous complete Middle English verse Troy romance of 18,664 lines, distinct from its unique witness and modern editions.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS. Laud Misc. 595
entities.description: The unique known manuscript witness; parchment, England, 1400-1410, with the poem on ff. 1r-275v and an early-fifteenth-century repaired binding.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian two-image selection from MS. Laud Misc. 595
entities.description: The exact current public object containing direct color images of fols. 1r and 87v only; complete relative to the provider object but partial relative to the codex and work.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF object cached as manifest plus two 1800-pixel JPEGs

entities.label: Wülfing edition printed phototype of MS. Laud Misc. 595 fol. 1r
entities.description: One indirect monochrome manuscript-image component printed in Part I and present in the U-M and Toronto Part I scans.
entities.reproduction_format: Printed phototype reproduced in page scans

entities.label: University of Toronto carrier of Wülfing Part I
entities.description: The physical Part I carrier represented by Internet Archive item laudtroybookroma00bodluoft; contains lines 1-10,876 only.

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of the Toronto Wülfing Part I carrier
entities.description: Complete 346-page provider scan of the physical Part I carrier; target lines 1-10,876 occur on PDF pages 21-340.

entities.label: University of Toronto carrier of Wülfing Part II
entities.description: The physical Part II carrier represented by Internet Archive item laudtroybookromapt200bodluoft; contains lines 10,877-18,664 only.

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of the Toronto Wülfing Part II carrier
entities.description: Complete 246-page provider scan of the physical Part II carrier; target lines 10,877-18,664 occur on PDF pages 13-241.

entities.label: Warburg Institute mirror/recode of the Toronto Wülfing Part II scan
entities.description: A smaller non-byte-identical 246-page PDF whose embedded metadata identifies the Toronto Internet Archive source item.

entities.label: Current EETS paperback reprint of The Laud Troy Book, OS 121-122
entities.description: Current purchasable paperback reprint route listed by EETS as ISBN 9780859916646; a reprint of Wülfing rather than a newly established text.

entities.label: Routledge Revivals reprint of The Laud Troy Book (2018)
entities.description: A separate modern commercial reprint in hardback, paperback, and ebook states, DOI 10.4324/9780429459184.

entities.label: Guido delle Colonne, Historia destructionis Troiae
entities.description: The Latin prose Troy history completed in 1287 and the confirmed direct source of The Laud Troy Book; the poet's exact physical exemplar is unknown.

entities.label: Benoît de Sainte-Maure, Roman de Troie
entities.description: The twelfth-century Old French verse romance, a possible direct source whose relationship to the Laud poem remains tentative because parallels may be mediated through Guido.

entities.label: DIMEV record-428 release snapshots (v1.0 and v2.0-alpha.1)
entities.description: A generalized catalog entity for exact record-428 as preserved in the separately evidenced DIMEV v1.0 and v2.0-alpha.1 release snapshots. Each release retains its own cached file, work portion, fixity, evidence, and rights scope; IMEV/NIMEV number 249 is not the DIMEV record identifier.

entities.label: Database of Middle English Romance work and MS. Laud Misc. 595 records
entities.description: Current linked work and manuscript descriptions used for date, tentative place, form, genre, themes, and witness context.

entities.label: J. Ernst Wülfing, Das Laud-Troybook (1901)
entities.description: Wülfing's 1901 scholarly article expression in Englische Studien 29, pp. 374-396, kept separate from the later journal reprint carrier and the modern Internet Archive facsimile.

entities.label: BNC Firenze 1477 incunable of Historia destructionis Troiae
entities.description: A complete early printed carrier of Guido's work used only as a supporting public representation, not as the identified exemplar of the Laud poet.

entities.label: Early European Books/Internet Archive scan of the BNC Firenze 1477 Guido incunable
entities.description: Complete 376-page provider scan; main text PDF pages 14-366 and table pages 367-369. Every image carries a ProQuest/Early European Books 2011 notice.

entities.label: Léopold Constans edition of Le Roman de Troie (1904-1912)
entities.description: Six-volume Société des anciens textes français edition: poem in volumes I-IV, notes/index/glossary in V, introduction in VI.

entities.label: University of Toronto six-volume Constans carrier set
entities.description: The Robarts Library physical set represented by the six Internet Archive scans.

entities.label: Internet Archive facsimile set of all six Constans volumes
entities.description: Six complete provider PDFs; all passed structural checks. Volumes I-IV contain the complete edited source work.

entities.label: Johnson Reprint Company carrier of Englische Studien 29
entities.description: The scanned physical journal-volume carrier reproduces the 1901 Englische Studien volume and bears a later Johnson Reprint Company permission/reprint statement; no reprint date was established. catalog_description is the current-schema carrier workaround.

entities.label: Internet Archive facsimile of the Englische Studien 29 reprint carrier
entities.description: A 482-page Internet Archive PDF created in 2024 from the Johnson Reprint Company carrier; Wülfing's article occupies journal pp. 374-396 / PDF pp. 382-404.

evidence.summary: Bibliographic source linking all six Constans volumes and related scholarship.

evidence.citation: Edmond Faral's Persée review record for Constans, Le Roman de Troie (1913).
evidence.summary: Independent bibliographic evidence for the Constans volume sequence and dates.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse v1.0, data/Records.xml record-428 (IMEV/NIMEV 249).
evidence.summary: The complete v1.0 release contains exact record-428 with the 18,664-line description, IMEV/NIMEV 249, unique witness locus, and Wülfing edition; the release LICENSE is CC BY 4.0.

evidence.citation: GitHub API latest-release response for the DIMEV repository.
evidence.summary: Modern GitHub release-wrapper metadata identifying v2.0-alpha.1 as the latest release on 2026-07-17; no independent reuse grant for the API response is asserted.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for Englische Studien 1901, volume 29.
evidence.summary: Identifies the modern Internet Archive wrapper and 2024 scan delivery separately from the 1901 article expression and the later Johnson Reprint Company carrier visible in the PDF.

open_questions.question: Does a complete public digital facsimile of MS. Laud Misc. 595 exist outside the exact current official search results?
next_steps: Recheck the exact Bodleian catalog and shelfmark search before future publication or ordering.

open_questions.question: What exact reuse terms would govern a future complete-codex reproduction order?
next_steps: Preserve the current contradictory forms and terms.
Obtain written permission covering public Git distribution and any connection to print-on-demand before reposting delivered images.
Create a new exact rights record for delivered files.

open_questions.question: What is the current physical folio extent of MS. Laud Misc. 595 for a whole-codex imaging quote?
next_steps: Do not treat Wülfing's 283-folio 1889 observation as the unverified current extent.
Ask the Bodleian to confirm current foliation and all photographable surfaces before calculating a price or delivery count.

open_questions.question: Did the Laud poet use Benoît directly or only through Guido?
next_steps: Keep relation:010 tentative at medium confidence.
Do not infer exemplar identity from the Constans edition.

open_questions.question: Was the CME Unlicense intended to cover source XML/data content or only software?
next_steps: Retain the current artifact rights status as unknown.
Do not use the software preamble as an unqualified XML content license.

review_notes: Primary transmission and supporting relations are kept separate; the U-M raw package contains the XML encoding and TIFF facsimile as parallel members.
Relations 001-009 are exhaustively represented in four explicit primary paths; every other relation appears exactly once in a supporting group.

The same 38,600,162-byte U-M raw ZIP is attached to its package, encoding, and TIFF-facsimile access routes, producing 54 local-copy occurrences from 52 unique paths while counting its stored bytes only once.
Wülfing's 1901 article expression, the later Johnson Reprint Company journal carrier, and the 2024 Internet Archive facsimile are modeled as separate layers.
The current EETS and Routledge products are catalog descriptions of mechanical reprints, not newly edited scholarly editions.
No complete public facsimile of MS. Laud Misc. 595 was found as of 2026-07-17; the cached Bodleian IIIF object is complete only for its two-image provider selection.

The manuscript, its modern images and delivery files, the medieval abstract works, scholarly editions, carriers, catalog records, and repository files have separate component- and access-scoped rights statements.
